summary: The adult skull comprises 22 bones: 8 cranial bones and 14 facial bones.
The adult skull comprises 20 bones: 6 cranial and 14 facial.
The adult skull comprises 28 bones: 8 cranial and 20 facial.
The pterion is the thinnest part of the skull and overlies the middle meningeal artery.
The pterion is the thickest part of the skull and protects the brain.
The pterion is a foramen through which the middle meningeal artery exits the skull.
The Frankfurt plane is defined by the lower orbital margin and upper border of the external acoustic meatus.
The Frankfurt plane passes through the nasion and inion.
The Frankfurt plane runs along the sagittal suture from bregma to lambda.
The foramen magnum transmits the brainstem (medulla oblongata), vertebral arteries, and spinal accessory nerve.
The foramen magnum transmits only the spinal cord and nothing else.
The foramen magnum transmits the internal carotid artery and the vagus nerve.
Intramembranous ossification forms flat bones of the skull vault (frontal, parietal) without a cartilage precursor.
Intramembranous ossification requires a cartilage template that is replaced by bone.
Intramembranous ossification is the process by which all skull base bones are formed.
